message:
Add full rule tracing to policy engine and surface-aware approval decision context

Policy engine (policy.rs): RuleEvaluationResult now tracks
matched_rule_ids — the set of ALL rules whose match_on criteria matched,
before priority/specificity tie-breaking selects the winner. The
PolicyEngine::evaluate method now records each config rule with its
actual match status, decision, and a trace reason explaining whether it
was selected or lost to a higher-priority rule. Previously all rules
were recorded as "rule checked" regardless of match status, making
audit logs opaque. The new config_rule_trace_reason helper generates
human-readable explanations like "rule matched but 'deny-ssh' won
tie-breaking: reason message".

The redundant record_rule calls in the Deny/RequireApproval/Allow
branches were removed since the loop above already records all rules
with correct match/decision info.

Approval system (main.rs): Added build_approval_decision_context which
constructs a full DecisionContext for approval-consume audit entries,
including actor kind (human/robot/mcp/workflow), the correct
PolicySurface for each actor type, the target action kind, pane ID,
and evidence fields. Previously the decision_context field was always
None in approval audit records, losing the chain of custody between
"who approved" and "what was approved". The evaluate_approve function
now populates this context on successful approval consumption.

Added approval_actor_kind and approval_target_action_kind helper
functions for safe enum parsing with sensible defaults.

New test: approve_records_surface_aware_decision_context_in_audit
verifies that human and robot actors produce the correct PolicySurface
in audit records.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
